key_outputs.verdict: DO NOT DEPLOY Dual Momentum - fails 5 of 7 deploy gates after realistic cost on real 2008-2026 data
key_outputs.dm_full: CAGR 6.05% maxDD 33.7% Sharpe 0.41 Calmar 0.18; OOS Sharpe 0.56 Calmar 0.26
key_outputs.benchmarks: SPY B&H Sharpe 0.68 (OOS 0.86); 60/40 Sharpe 0.77 (OOS 0.89) maxDD 29.7%/21.7% near-zero tax - 60/40 dominates the active designs risk-adjusted AND after-tax
key_outputs.control_a: rotation: lowest maxDD 14.8% but worst tax drag ~43% and Sharpe 0.57 < 60/40; also not worth deploying
key_outputs.regimes: DM edge is GFC-only; lost COVID 2020 (-24.7% vs SPY -3.9%, V-reversal), 2022 (-20.2%), chop 2015-16 (-5.3% vs +5.2%)
key_outputs.montecarlo: median maxDD ~40%, median Sharpe 0.44, p5 CAGR ~0% - typical path also fails
key_outputs.sensitivity: no config in lookback{126..315}xtopk{1,2} beats the benchmarks; 252-default sub-optimal
key_outputs.recommendation: simpler approach wins: a low-turnover 60/40-style allocation. Either return to Stage 1 for a static/low-turnover sleeve, or add no ETF sleeve. DM stays OFF by default.

assumptions: 5 bps/side slippage, commission-free, fills at next open (gap-aware)
taxable account (Stage-0): tax drag estimate weighted heavily
yfinance research-only data; warmup excludes the 2007 top (worst of GFC included)
open_issues: human sign-off on the negative verdict (mandatory, high financial impact)
decide: return to Stage 1 for a simple allocation, or stop and add no ETF sleeve
Stages 5-6 (paper deploy / go-live) only proceed for a design that PASSES - dual momentum does not
handoff.next_focus: Human decision: the data says deploy a simple low-turnover allocation (60/40-style) or nothing. Do NOT paper-deploy dual momentum.

correctness.reasoning: Harness pure functions unit-tested (cost/metrics/sim/benchmarks/tax/bootstrap); simulator verified to fill at next open + model gaps; benchmark CAGRs (SPY 12.2%, 60/40 8.95%) are plausible for the period.

methodological_rigor.reasoning: Walk-forward IS/OOS, 4 regimes, block-bootstrap distribution, parameter sensitivity, realistic cost + gap-at-open, after-tax estimate, vs SPY and 60/40 and the incumbent control - all required elements present.

robustness.reasoning: Verdict survives multiple independent gates (G1/G2/G5/G6/G7) and the full sensitivity grid; not a single-metric or single-path call.

safety_compliance.reasoning: Research-only (no trading); yfinance isolated as a research dep; dual momentum stays OFF by default; honest negative verdict, no rationalizing a weak result.

simplicity.reasoning: Harness is cohesive and pure; the recommendation explicitly favors the simplest winning approach (60/40) over added complexity.

clarity.reasoning: Report quantifies every gate, names the failure modes (V-reversal, 2022 bonds+stocks, chop whipsaw), and states the recommendation plainly with honest limitations.

issues_found: Tax model is a simplified estimate (relative comparison only)
252-warmup excludes the 2007 top (does not change the verdict)
suggested_revisions: Mandatory human sign-off on the negative verdict and the choice of next step (simple allocation vs no sleeve)
